steps.title: Find the axis
steps.content:
$f(-12)=f(4)$ means those two $x$-values are symmetric around the vertex. The axis of symmetry is
$$\frac{-12+4}{2}=-4$$

steps.title: Find the value of a
steps.content:
For $f(x)=ax^2+5x+c$, the axis is
$$x=-\frac{5}{2a}$$
Set it equal to $-4$.
$$-\frac{5}{2a}=-4$$
$$a=\frac{5}{8}$$
So statement II does not have to be true.

steps.title: Test the value of c
steps.content: The vertex value is $$k=f(-4)=\frac{5}{8}(16)+5(-4)+c=c-10$$ Since $k<0$, only $c<10$ is required. For example, $c=1$ works and is not less than $0$, so statement I does not have to be true. The answer is D.
